observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to
provide the Municipal Accountant the fair value less cost to sell of the biological
assets and documents of the monitoring made pertaining to the dispersal, birth,
and death of the biological assets which will be the basis of its recording.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to continue enforcing available remedies regarding the
collection of taxes and its corresponding penalties; and
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records and
make the necessary adjustments, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: ● Disposal Committee/GSO to dispose the unserviceable properties following the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and ● GSO to submit the supporting documents and additional information required for request for relief from property accountability to facilitate its evaluation. We also recommended to create an Inventory Committee for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances following the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-06.
observations.managementAction: The Disposal Committee/GSO to dispose the unserviceable properties is still conducting the physical count/inspection. The GSO submitted the additional documents for evaluation. There was an Inventory Committee created.
observations.reason: The non-completion was due to a series of typhoons that beset the Municipality which affected the conducting the physical count/inspection of the unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to complete the assessment roll for the remaining 38 barangays and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the list of taxpayers manually while it could not be provided yet by the RPTAS. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer follow up the status of the formulation by the SB of ordinance adopting the remedies provided in RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Assessor has completed the assessment roll of the remaining 38 barangays. The list of taxpayers was prepared and submitted to the Audit Team. Follow-ups were made regarding the matter.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Appraisal Committee to determine/appraise the fair value of the 14 parcels of land at the time of acquisition to be used as basis in the adjustment of the book balance; and the Municipal Assessor to secure from the RD the copy of owner’s title of the two parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: The Appraisal Committee has appraised the fair value of the 14 parcels of land. Also, the Assessor has coordinated with the RD with regard to the owner’s title.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue the confirmation from the claimants and after the lapse of the prescribed period to claim, determine the accounts that can be reverted already to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to the authority from the SB with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that a claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction: All outstanding balances were reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to exert more effort to complete the necessary information and documents relevant to the deaths/sale of animals; demand from the accountable beneficiaries the refund of the book value of animals which death/sale was unjustified and undocumented; and file with the Commission on Audit (COA) the request for relief from accountability together with the required supporting documents, so that derecognition of these from the books could be effected once the request is granted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist submitted a report with regard to the death/sale of animals.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Market Supervisor to revisit the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises and comply with the guidelines provided thereon; fast track regular review of the proposed revised Market Code and enactment; formulate and implement strategies to improve the financial performance of the LMEEU; and coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the immediate collection of the unpaid electric bills of the market stall owners to avoid accumulation of receivables.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Market Supervisor has revisited the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises and ensured that they comply with its guidelines. There is also a fast track regular collection of the unpaid electric bills to avoid accumulation of receivables.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer, MSWDO and the Municipal Agriculturist to exert more effort and enhance the existing income sources and collection strategies to encourage debtors to pay their loans. We also reiterated our recommendation to file to the Commission on Audit (COA) the request for authority to write-off uncollectible/dormant accounts due to death and unknown whereabouts of the debtors, following the procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has filed for the request for authority to write-off uncollectible/dormant accounts due to death and unknown whereabouts of the debtors.
observations.reason: The partial implementation was due to the COVID-19 pandemic which greatly affected income sources of the beneficiaries and their capabilities to pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Computer Programmer to continue fixing the computer system defects by requiring the Service Provider to complete its overdue obligation and providing funds for upgrading and preventive maintenance.
observations.managementAction: The Computer Programmer was able to fix the majority of the system. The upgrade is still underway.
observations.reason: Funding to fix the remaining issues and/or its upgrade is still underway.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Agriculturist to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer, OIC-MPDC and source agencies for the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Four out of five projects were implemented by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still completing the documentary requirements for the unimplemented projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and the OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to exert more effort and strategize the implementation of the programmed PPAs for 20 percent DF to ensure timely execution and completion of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the current and continuing development projects were implemented in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit an explanation for the deficiencies noted in the technical evaluation of the infrastructure projects. Moreover, ensure that the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) is in accordance with Section 31.1 of the IRR of RA 9184 and DPWH DO No. 197 series 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has submitted an explanation for the deficiencies noted in the infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the SWM Board expedite the review of the 10-Year SWM Plan and submit the same to the NSWMC for approval, in compliance with Section 16 of RA No. 9003. We also recommended to convene the SWM Board and conduct face to face meeting or virtual meeting if face to face meeting is not possible.
observations.managementAction: SWM was able to draft the 10-Year SWM Plan. Face to face meetings were called whenever possible.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Service Provider to fix the noted gaps on the Computerization Program. We also recommended to provide budget for the upgrading and preventive maintenance of its software and hardware components to maximize its full capacity.
observations.managementAction: The over-all progress of the repair/fixing of the computer system by the Service Provider is around 80-90 percent already. The budget for the preventive maintenance was allocated for each system depending on its needs and status. Canvass for the total cost of upgrading is on-going.
observations.reason: The needed upgrade details were still for preparation and the budget for the preventive maintenance was still for allocation.

review of the 10-Year SWM Plan and submit the same to the NSWMC for
approval, in compliance with Section 16 of RA No. 9003. We also recommend
to convene the SWM Board through virtual meeting if face to face meeting is
not possible.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Disposal Committee/General Services Officer (GSO) to dispose the unserviceable properties following a specific timeline and the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; • Municipal Agriculturist to prepare the inventory reports of distributed agricultural equipment to barangays and submit the same to the GSO/Inventory Committee.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property was prepared and submitted to the Commission on Audit Audit Team. The inventory reports were prepared and submitted to the GSO/Inventory Committee. The inventory and status reports were prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The supporting documents were still not complete as of the end of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to prepare the list manually or adopt other alternative means to generate the list of taxpayers while it could not be provided yet by the system. We also recommended to follow up the status of the request to the Sangguniang Bayan on the enactment of an ordinance adopting the remedies provided in RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The manual list for the 57 Barangays was already on-going. The manual list is also necessary in the future to test the accuracy of the system-generated list. Drafting of applicable ordinance is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Appraisal Committee to determine/appraise the fair value of the 14 parcels of land at the time of acquisition to be used as basis in the adjustment of the book balance; and the Municipal Assessor to secure from the RD the copy of owner’s title of the two parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: The Deeds of Donation and Sale of three lots were already secured. A letter was sent to the RD in January and April 2021 requesting for certified copies of any documents/mode of conveyance for the nine parcels of land and title of the two parcels of land.
observations.reason: Due to lack of records, specifically the Deed of Conveyance; and book and page number as basis for the request.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO submit immediately the RPCI to support the year-end balance of Inventories of P1,733,344.50 and the Municipal Accountant to record the appropriate expense accounts for the issuance of inventories out of the beginning balances.
observations.managementAction: The RPCI was submitted to the Audit Team and the appropriate expense accounts for the issuance of inventories was recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445, subject to the authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that a claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction: Letters were sent to the claimants informing them to withdraw their bidders/performance bond or retention money within 15 days before requesting authority from the SB for the reversion.
observations.reason: Sending of confirmation letters was not completed due to unknown addresses of the claimants and limitations caused by the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Market Supervisor to revisit the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises and comply with the guidelines provided thereon; fast track the review of the proposed revised Market Code for SB enactment; and formulate and implement strategies to improve the financial performance of the LMEEU. We also recommended to observe proper charging of expenses for fair reporting and cause the immediate collection of the unpaid electric bills of the market stall owners to avoid accumulation of receivables.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Market Supervisor prepared the Financial Self-Sufficiency Evaluation (FSSE) Report from CY 2014-2020 and revisited the Manual. They are working to facilitate the collection of stall and table rentals.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower and the pandemic. Delayed SB enactment of the Revised Market Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer, MSWDO and the Municipal Agriculturist to exert more effort and enhance the existing collection strategies to encourage debtors to pay their loans. We also recommended to consider the filing of the request for authority to write-off of uncollectible/dormant accounts due to death and unknown whereabouts of the debtors, following the procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is coordinating with Barangay Officials and personally contacting debtors. The MSWDO will file a request for authority to write-off for 14 uncollectible accounts due to death and the Municipal Agriculturist sustained sending of collection letters and prepared request for authority to write-off.
observations.reason: Collection efforts were hampered by the COVID-19 pandemic and some necessary supporting documents for write-off were lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to exert more effort to complete the necessary information and documents relevant to the deaths of animals; update the inventory and status report based on the gathered documents and submit the same to the Accounting Office and Audit Team; and file immediately with the Audit Team the request for relief from accountability as provided under Section 73 of PD No. 1445 and COA Memorandum No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the MAO and provided detailed listings. The MAO agreed to submit the revised Inventory and Status Report of Animal Dispersal. The MAO stated that supporting documents were submitted only to the Municipal Accountant and amendments in the MOA will be proposed. They are trying to adopt monitoring measures despite limited staff.
observations.reason: Only one staff is assigned in the monitoring of animals; the needed documentation was not completed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to submit the monthly monitoring reports regularly and inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the project that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices were already posted.
observations.managementAction: The monthly monitoring report was submitted regularly and the Audit Team was informed that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the concerned implementing office to coordinate with the source agencies for the implementation of the projects; and advise the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the unexpended balances to the source agencies/National Treasury if the projects are already completed.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MPDC, Municipal Agriculturist and Municipal Engineer prepared a catch-up action plan for the projects that will be implemented. They are considering returning the funds for the Community Fish Landing Center due to ECC issues.
observations.reason: Not implemented due to various planning and implementation issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the OIC-MPDC to exert more effort in the implementation of the planned PPAs for 20 percent DF to ensure timely execution and fully achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes within the target period.
observations.managementAction: Several projects were implemented and completed during the year; the OME is working to take steps to implement the projects and catch up with preparation and revision of Detailed Engineering Designs since the second half of the year.
observations.reason: Some projects were delayed due to rebidding, cutting of works, DOLE-approved Construction Safety and Health Program issues and pandemic-related delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Local DRRM Officer to ensure that the DRRM PPAs are implemented within the target schedule during the year to maximize the utilization of LDRRMF by proper planning and monitoring, minimize the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the community.
observations.managementAction: The DRRM Officer prioritized the implementation of DRRM PPAs for the year and reported actions to implement projects as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the SWM Board ensure compliance with RA No. 9003 by expediting review and approval of the 10-Year SWM Plan and acquisition of sanitary landfill site and implementing SWM activities and campaigns towards a cleaner community and environment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already acquired a lot for its sanitary landfill site and titling was completed. The SWM Board convened and provided comments; finalization of the 10-Year SWMP is being expedited for submission.
observations.reason: Members of the SWM Board failed to convene during the year due to restrictions on mass gatherings caused by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to enhance and strengthen the existing monitoring and evaluation system to ensure that the programmed GAD PPAs are fully implemented and the GAD funds are fully utilized within the year; include a training program on the use of the HGDG in the GPB for the succeeding year and seek the assistance of a gender analysis specialist/expert in the use of and administration of the HGDG.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented 28 GAD projects out of 62 programmed activities and concurred with the recommendations to enhance monitoring and strengthen the GAD implementation.
observations.reason: PPAs with a total appropriation of P2,155,000.00 which pertain to trainings, seminars, meetings and assemblies were not implemented due to restrictions on mass gatherings caused by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Disposal Committee/General Services Officer (GSO) to dispose the
unserviceable properties following a specific timeline and the guidelines
provided under Commission on Audit Circular No. 92-386 dated October
20, 1992;
• Municipal Agriculturist to prepare the inventory reports of distributed
agricultural equipment to barangays and submit the same to the Inventory
Committee/GSO for verification;
• GSO to submit to the Municipal Accountant the supporting
documents/Deed of Donation for the donated motor vehicles;
• Municipal Accountant to derecognize the donated motor vehicles and
record the same in the barangay books; and
• Municipal Accountant and the GSO to designate personnel from both
offices who will concentrate on identification and disposition of the
unaccounted PPE and reconciliation of their records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Assessor to prepare the list manually or adopt other
alternative means to generate the list of taxpayers while it could not be
provided yet by the system. The manual list is also necessary in the future to
test the accuracy of the system-generated list.
We also recommended to follow up the status of the request to the SB on the
enactment of an ordinance adopting the remedies provided in RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Appraisal Committee
to determine/appraise the fair value of the 14 parcels of land at the time of
acquisition to be used as basis in the adjustment of the book balance; and the
Municipal Assessor to secure from the RD the copy of owner’s title of the two
parcels of land.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to submit
immediately the RPCI to support the year-end balance of Inventories of
P1,733,344.50 and the Municipal Accountant to record the appropriate expense
accounts for the issuance of inventories out of the beginning balances of
Inventory accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund
pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445, subject to the authority
from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) with a condition that appropriation shall be
made available in the event that a claim arises in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Market Supervisor
to revisit the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic
Enterprises and comply with the guidelines provided thereon, fast track the
review of the proposed revised Market Code and submit the same to the SB for
enactment; and formulate and implement strategies to improve the financial
performance of the LMEEU.
We also recommended to observe proper charging of expenses for fair
reporting and cause the immediate collection of the unpaid electric bills of the
market stall owners to avoid accumulation of receivables.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Service Provider to fix the noted gaps on the Computerization Program. We
also recommended to provide budget for the upgrading and preventive
maintenance of its software and hardware components to maximize its full
capacity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer,
MSWDO and the Municipal Agriculturist to exert more effort and enhance the
existing collection strategies to encourage debtors to pay their loans.
We also recommended to consider the filing of the request for authority to
write-off of uncollectible/dormant accounts due to death and unknown
whereabouts of the debtors, following the procedures provided under
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist
to exert more effort to complete the necessary information and documents
relevant to the deaths of animals; update the inventory and status report
based on the gathered documents and submit the same to the Accounting
Office for reconciliation/adjustment; and cause the refund of the book value or
derecognition of the account amounting to P339,265.00.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist, with respect to
death of animals, file immediately with the Audit Team the request for relief
from accountability as provided under Section 73 of Presidential Decree
No. 1445 and COA Memorandum No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Engineer to submit the monthly monitoring reports regularly and
inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices were already posted.

a) Issue an office order creating the new Inventory Committee (MANCOM) and Appraisal Committee who will conduct the actual physical count of the Municipality’s property and will appraise the PPE which has no available information regarding its acquisition date and cost, respectively; b) Constantly monitor the status of these activities to ensure completion and the submission of complete RPCPPE within the set deadline indicated in the office order; c) Require the accountable officer to pay the cost of PPE sent which were found missing and has no request for relief from accountability, if any; or replace the lost PPE. d) Require the GSO to ensure that all PPE counted by the Inventory Committee are issued with PAR and provided with PC, and to regularly update the same; e) Require the GSO to stick with property tag all counted PPE and ensure to stick it to all newly acquired PPE before its release and issuance/release to end-user; f) Require the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to identify/locate the unaccounted PPE in the GF and SEF totaling P5,428,429.46 and P23,364.00, respectively.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO commented through her letter dated August 3, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor, that she will try her best to obtain the notarized Deed of Donation as soon as possible and to locate/identify the unaccounted PPE by delegating a knowledgeable staff who will concentrate on the task. She also informed that she already communicated with the Municipal Agriculturist on the distributed agricultural equipment while revision/updating of the IIRUP for disposal purposes is ongoing.
The Municipal Accountant commented through her letter dated July 30, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor, that it is their utmost desire to derecognize the cost of unserviceable properties but due to non-disposal, derecognition could not yet be effected. With regards to the donated PPE to the barangays, the related documents are still for submission to her Office. She also stated that tracing and reconciliation of the unaccounted PPE shall be given due attention through designation of dedicated personnel who will focus on the task.

observations.recommendation: a) Require the MT to follow up his request to the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan on the status of the request. b) Require the MT to prepare the list of individual taxpayers with delinquencies for years 2002 to 2018 as of December 31, 2018 and furnish it to the Municipal Accountant; c) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments of RPT/SET Receivables once the list of delinquent taxpayers is received from the MT to reflect the correct balance of these receivable accounts; and d) Instruct the MT to ensure that the submitted reports to the Audit Team are tally with the other records or with the computer system of his Office.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Assessor commented through her letter dated June 24, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that their Office is presently preparing the Assessment Roll of 95 Barangays pertaining to 2019 General Revision of Real Properties with more or less 30,000 Real Property Units (RPUs). To date, they have accomplished 50 Barangays and committed that they will try their best to submit the remaining 45 Barangays to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office until October 31, 2020.
The Municipal Treasurer also commented through his letter dated June 8, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that he already provided the Accounting Office with the list of delinquent taxpayers. Further, he stated that per recent consultation with the SB Secretary on the enactment of ordinance, it was informed that instead of enforcing collection, giving of incentives to Barangays which will record high property tax collections, is being considered.
observations.reason: Giving of incentives was considered.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the GSO with the necessary support in enjoining all barangays to establish their own MRF and to strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003. (Note: this appears to be from a different observation — please refer to the actual table row's recommendation content)
(For inventories specifically:) The Municipal Mayor to require the Inventory Committee, GSO and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the regulations/procedures on the proper accounting, recording and subsequent management of purchase inventories in accordance with the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs in order to have reliable balances of inventories and relative expenses and to avoid improper utilization and wastage of the same. Also recommend GSO be provided with stock room to facilitate compliance; prepare RPCI and update records; and segregate prior year issuances properly.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant commented through her letter dated June 15, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that issuance of inventories pertaining to prior year’s purchases were charged to PPA account to segregate it from current year’s purchases. According to her, although the expense was understated by the amount of expenses charged to PPA, eventually it was closed to Government Equity account, same with Expense account. Nevertheless, she agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.
The GSO submitted the RPCI and explained through her letter dated August 3, 2020 that due to frequent movement of employees and voluminous workload in their Office, they were not able to submit to the Accounting Office the required reports on time or before closing of the books. Further, she stated that she will delegate the preparation of inventory records and reports to a permanent and knowledgeable staff.
observations.reason: Plan of total renovation of the Municipal Building. GSO compliance ongoing; RPCI submission delayed due to workload.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to record the one parcel of land at its market value pending determination of its fair value; the Appraisal committee/team to properly appraise the donated land as to their fair values at the time of acquisition and apply the same to the purchased land if the acquisition costs cannot be determined reliably; the Municipal Assessor to follow up the request to CENRO to facilitate the titling of the four parcels of land in the name of the Municipality; and the GSO to follow-up the request from the RD regarding the owner’s copy of title for the six parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor submitted a revised list of the land owned by the Municipality showing revised market values with declaration dates through her letter dated July 29, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor, and commented that due to lack of records such as the Deed of Donation/Sale, correct appraisal of the lands at their acquisition dates was not facilitated. Nevertheless, she committed that the Appraisal Committee will try their best to come up with the appraised fair values at the time of acquisition and will seek assistance of other government agencies to trace and gather additional records of the properties, for reference.
observations.reason: The 14 parcels of land were still recorded at their market values; revised zonal values and current market values influenced appraisals.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials concerned to address the causes of the incurrence of operating losses in the operation of the Municipal Public Market and Municipal Slaughterhouse; that the Municipal Mayor require strict compliance with the regulations regarding the budgeting and accounting for proper and fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements (FS); and to ensure that costs of improvements and insurance of Public Market are properly charged to the LMEEU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor commented through her letter dated July 18, 2020 that the OIC-Market Supervisor was just appointed last year but has shown her commitment to implement innovations in the operation of LMEEU. She also assured that they will comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Operation still incurred operating loss; insurance and some costs were charged to General Fund; ongoing compliance and market code revision pending.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Service Provider (SP) to fix the noted gaps on the Computerization Program- Phases I and II, otherwise the total payments of P7,008,891.00 will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Computer Programmer commented through his letter dated August 3, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that the Service Provider is doing its best to provide the needs of the end-users. The Municipal Mayor will coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the provision of the budget for the upgrading and maintenance of the systems.
observations.reason: Fixing of gaps by the SP is still ongoing; lack of preventive maintenance and upgrades contributed to issues.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist: a) Complete the conduct of the physical count/inventory of the animals and prepare and render the report thereon complete with supporting documents and as proof of the status/condition of animals; b) Conduct a complete investigation on the circumstances surrounding the death of the animals and if negligence was committed, compel the concerned recipients to refund the cost of animals for replacement; c) Make a detailed monitoring of the status of the animals and its beneficiaries; d) Prove with evidence the allegation of the non-receipt of swine by beneficiaries of dispersal from 2001 to 2004; and e) Exert effort to find the corresponding Animal Dispersal Contract for all animals dispersed and corresponding the recorded cost of animals.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require Municipal Agriculturist to evaluate the effectiveness of the animal dispersal before implementing similar projects; conduct seminars/trainings to intended beneficiaries; and Municipal Treasurer to collect the cost of the animals from beneficiaries in accordance with the dispersal contract.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were not available; difficulty in retrieval of old records and lack of monitoring staff affected full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the concerned suppliers to refund the amount of P9,194.85 as LD, otherwise the former should be made personally liable. Also reiterated that LD should be recognized and deducted before payments are made to suppliers on the account of procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: The LD of P9,194.85 was refunded by the supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Provide the GSO with sufficient storage for minor unserviceable PPE and a covered storage for unserviceable motor vehicles, heavy equipment and other large items to protect them and avoid fast deterioration; b) Require the GSO to prepare a separate IIRUP for those with complete documents and thereafter require her to exert efforts to locate its documents; c) Require the GSO to ensure that all unserviceable PPE reported in IIRUP are existing; and d) Require the GSO to stick with property tag all counted PPE and ensure to stick it to all newly acquired PPE before its release and issuance/release to end-user (this last sub-recommendation repeated from PPE items).
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant commented through her letter dated June 11, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that the recorded payables were based on actual collections and deductions from suppliers’ claims. She added that these were not automatically reverted after two years because the creditors/claimants might, at anytime, present their claims considering that these are trust liabilities recorded in the books and the account balance should not be considered questionable or unreliable. Nonetheless, she agreed to secure authority from the SB for the reversion of the accounts to the GF but only after receiving the confirmation replies from the suppliers/contractors and upon presentation of supporting documents.
The GSO submitted the IIRUP and explained through her letter dated August 3, 2020 that due to frequent movement of employees and voluminous workload in their Office, they were not able to submit to the Accounting Office the required reports on time or before closing of the books. Further, she stated that she will delegate the preparation of inventory records and reports to a permanent and knowledgeable staff.
observations.reason: Lack of enough storage area during the year; time-consuming reconstruction of previous IIRUP; many records/documentation beyond recovery; GSO efforts ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MT, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) and Municipal Agriculturist to continue adopting efficient and aggressive collection and monitoring strategies to enforce collection of the loan receivable accounts from concerned debtors; distribute demand letters; update the status report; sustain farm and home visits; and consider filing request for authority to write-off uncollectible/dormant accounts as appropriate.
observations.managementAction:
The MSWDO commented through her letter dated August 3, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that her Office had been in continuous effort to collect the receivables from livelihood project beneficiaries as they were able to collect P12,903.00 as of first semester of current year while 34 debtors committed to settle their loans on installment payment. She also estimated that at least 35 percent increase from previous year’s rate shall be collected by the end of calendar year 2020. For uncollectible accounts due to death and unknown whereabouts, they will file the request for authority to write-off with COA.
As for the Municipal Agriculturist, she commented through her letter dated July 25, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that they will continue reminding the beneficiaries of their obligations and recommend to the Municipal Mayor the filing with COA the request for authority to write-off accounts pertaining to the deceased borrowers.
observations.reason: Deceased beneficiaries/unknown whereabouts and lack of supporting documents for some accounts; collection efforts ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor strictly comply with CSC Resolution No. 021480 and use Contract of Service or JO instead of Appointment Paper; and require from JOs the submission of documents pertaining to educational background, work experience and trainings attended as stated in their PDS/Bio-Data, including the clearances from criminal records secured from the NBI or PNP and concerned Barangay Clearance, to establish their identity and suitability to the job.
observations.managementAction: The new OIC-Municipal HRMO supported the request of the Municipal Mayor and implemented CSC Resolution No. 021480. Contracts of Service were issued from July 1, 2019 onwards. The COS were supported with Police Clearance, diploma and photocopy of valid ID.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the head of the concerned implementing offices to submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year under review; and submit the monthly monitoring report. Also required that the Municipal Mayor inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer commented through his letter dated July 15, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that the contractors were not complying with the posting and proper installation of signboards. He also stated that they have already hired three new licensed engineers who will assist in the monitoring and inspection of the project accomplishments but cited that due to distant location of some projects, lack of service vehicles and safety issues in some areas, they could not easily access the project sites for monthly report updating.
observations.reason: Delayed posting of project signboards by contractors; accessibility and resource constraints affected monthly reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to find solutions to the problems that are causing delays in the implementation of development projects; and require the MPDC to monitor the implementation of all projects programmed under the 20 percent Development Fund so that intended beneficiaries avail the maximum benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and the OIC-MPDC commented through their letter dated June 24, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that to address the accumulation of unimplemented projects, the Office of the Municipal Engineer augmented the workforce by hiring one Licensed Civil Engineer and two Non-Licensed Engineers under Contract of Service to ensure that the necessary documents such as Detailed Engineering Design and Program of Work are ready prior to project implementation. In addition, the OIC-MPDC made an internal arrangement by assigning one of its technical personnel to focus on the monitoring of all PPAs who would prepare and update report, countercheck the submitted accomplishment report of the concerned implementing office/unit, recommend necessary measures and coordinate with/remind the implementing office/unit to fast track their project implementation.
observations.reason: Voluminous workload; contractors' difficulty securing DOLE-approved CSHP; suspension due to unpassable roads; termination of contracts due to non-compliance.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO commented through his letter dated June 24, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that the LDRRMF was not maximized during the year due to non-availability of permanent evacuation center and procurement issues, specifically the interpretation of existing procurement guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the SWM Board ensure the Municipality’s compliance with RA No. 9003 by expediting the review and approval of the 10-Year SWM Plan and acquisition of sanitary landfill site and implementing more SWM activities and campaigns towards a cleaner community and environment.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO-Designate commented through his letter dated June 8, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that there was an initial conduct of Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS) and a team was created to draft the SWM Plan, however the finalization of the plan was not completed this year due to heavy workload in their Office. Moreover, he stated that the acquisition of sanitary landfill site was subjected to Road Right of Way ownership issues. Fortunately, this was already resolved and they are now processing the required documents.
observations.reason: SWM Plan preparation and sanitary landfill acquisition ongoing; activities limited due to workload and pending documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to strictly comply with the guidelines in preparing a correct GPB and GAR and to submit to the Audit Team a copy of the Annual GAD AR within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year for proper and timely validation of the Municipality’s GAD accomplishments and actual expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person commented through her letter dated July 13, 2020 which was noted by the Municipal Mayor that starting this year, she will ensure the full implementation of GAD PPAs to address the different gender issues, as mandated under the Magna Carta for Women; and will request the Provincial GAD Office for a HGDG training intended for GAD Unit and MGFPS Council.
observations.reason: Lack of awareness/training on HGDG and reporting requirements; planned training requested from Provincial GAD Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the head of the concerned implementing offices to: a) submit the list of all on-going government PPAs of the immediately preceding year and those that are to be implemented during the year under review; and the monthly monitoring report; and b) inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted to facilitate validation by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: MPDC committed to comply. Municipal Engineer submitted schedule of completed and on-going projects for 2018 on March 8, 2019 and explained some contractors did not comply with posting of signboards due to security/theft issues. Committed to comply.
observations.reason: Overlooked; submission was not made at the beginning of the year as required; schedule submitted after year-end is not compliant with the regulation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Issue an office order creating the new Inventory Committee and Appraisal
Committee who will conduct the actual physical count of the Municipality’s
property and will appraise the PPE which has no available information
regarding its acquisition date and cost, respectively;
b) Constantly monitor the status of these activities to ensure completion and
submission of complete RPCPPE within the set deadline indicated in the office
order;
c) Require the accountable officer to pay the cost of PPE which were found
missing and has no request for relief from accountability, if any;
d) Require the MGSO to ensure that all PPE counted by the Inventory Committee
are issued with PAR and provided with PC, and to regularly update the same;
e) Require the MGSO to stick with property tag all counted PPE and ensure to
stick it to all newly acquired PPE before its release; and
f) Require the MGSO and Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to
identify/locate the unaccounted PPE in GF and SEF totaling P5,428,429.46 and
P23,364.00, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the MT to follow up his request to the Office of the SB in order for the
latter to create an ordinance adopting the remedies provided in RA No. 7160 so
as to enforce the collection of delinquent taxes;
b) Require the MT to prepare the list of individual taxpayers with delinquencies
for years 2002 to 2018 as of December 31, 2018 and furnish it to the Municipal
Accountant;
c) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments of
RPT/SET Receivables once the list of delinquent taxpayers is received from the
MT to reflect the correct balance of these receivable accounts; and
d) Instruct the MT to ensure that the submitted reports to the Audit Team are tally
with the other records or with the computer system of his Office.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory
Committee, MGSO and Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the
regulations/procedures on the proper accounting, recording and management of
inventories in accordance with the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs in order to have
reliable balances of inventories and relative expenses and to avoid improper
utilization and wastage of the same.
We further reiteratedour recommendation that the Municipal Mayor provide the
MGSO with stock room to facilitate compliance with the regulation on proper
recording and reporting of the receipt and disposal of inventories.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant to record the one parcel of land at its market value of
P1,705,650.00 pending determination of its fair value;
Appraisal committee/team to properly appraise the donated land as to their fair
values at the time of acquisition and apply the same to the purchased land if
the acquisition costs cannot be determined reliably;
Municipal Assessor to follow up the request from CENRO to facilitate the
titling of the four parcels of land in the name of the Municipality; and
MGSO to follow-up the request from the RD regarding the owner’s copy of title
for the six parcels of land.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials
concerned to address the causes of the incurrence of operating losses in the
operation of the Municipal Public Market and Municipal Slaughterhouse.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate strict compliance with
the regulations regarding the budgeting and accounting for expenses of economic
enterprises for proper and fair presentation of accounts in the financial
statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the service
provider to fix the noted gaps on Computerization Program-Phases I and II,
otherwise the total payments of P7,008,891.00 will be disallowed in audit.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Agriculturist to:
a) Complete the conduct of the physical count/inventory of the animals and
prepare and render the report thereon complete with supporting documents as
proof of the status/condition of animals;
b) Conduct a complete investigation on the circumstances surrounding the death
of the animals and if negligence was committed, compel the concerned
recipients to refund the cost of animals;
c) Make a detailed monitoring of the animals and its beneficiaries;
d) Prove with evidence the allegation of the non-receipt of 22 swine by
beneficiaries of dispersal from 2001 to 2004; and
e) Exert effort to find the corresponding Animal Dispersal Contract for all animals
dispersed corresponding the recorded cost of animals.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Agriculturist make an evaluation/assessment of the effectiveness of
the animal dispersal before implementing similar projects;
b) Municipal Agriculturist to conduct seminars/trainings to the intended
beneficiaries before implementing the projects and afterwards assist them in
the proper care of the animals; and
c) Municipal Treasurer to collect the cost of the animals from its beneficiaries in
accordance with the dispersal contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
require the concerned suppliers to refund the amount of P9,194.85 as LD,
otherwise the former should be made personally liable.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that the LD in future transactions, if any, are duly
recognized and deducted before payments are made to suppliers on the account of its
procurement activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor;
a) Provide the MGSO with sufficient storage for minor unserviceable PPE and a
covered space for unserviceable motor vehicles, heavy equipment and other
large items to protect them and avoid fast deterioration;
b) Require the MGSO to prepare a separate IIRUP for those with complete
documents and prioritize its disposal thru sale by public auction or other modes
of disposal to earn at least the salvage value of these assets and to unclog the very
limited office space/storage of the Municipality;
c) Require the MGSO to prepare another IIRUP for those that can be individually
identified but with incomplete documents and thereafter require her to exert
efforts to locate its documents; and
d) Require the MGSO to ensure that all unserviceable PPE reported in IIRUP are
existing.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MT,
Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officerand Municipal Agriculturist to
continue adopting efficient and aggressive collection strategies to enforce
collection of the loan receivable accounts from the concerned debtors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly comply with CSC Resolution No.
021480 and use the Contract of Service or JO instead of Appointment Paper.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the HRMO to require
from JOs the submission of documents pertaining to educational background,
work experience and trainings attended as stated in their PDS/Bio-Data, including
the clearances from criminal records secured from the NBI or PNP and concerned
Barangay to establish their identity and suitability to the job applied for.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the head of
the concerned implementing offices to submit the list of all on-going government
PPAs of the immediately preceding year and those that are to be implemented
during the year under review; and the monthly monitoring report.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor inform the Audit
Team within ten days after the award of the project or before the start of the
program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are
already posted to facilitate validation by the Audit Team.

For reliable PPE balances, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct and complete the physical count of the Municipality’s property and submit the Report on the Physical count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Inventory and Inspection of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for GF, Special Education Fund and Lopez Municipal Economic Enterprises Unit (LMEEU) to the Audit Team; b) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to reconcile their records and reports, exert effort to identify the unaccounted PPE located by locating their records/reports; c) require the MGSO to maintain complete and updated Property Cards and renew the PARs every three years; d) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Schedule of Accumulated Depreciation and Other Assets (for unserviceable PPE) which had matched to the total recorded PPE; and e) provide depreciation for Other Land Improvements account of GF.
observations.managementAction: Conducted physical count of PPE and submitted the RPCPPE. MGSO. Municipality’s property and submit the Report on the Physical count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Inventory and Inspection of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for GF, Special Education Fund and Lopez Municipal Economic Enterprises Unit (LMEEU) to the Audit Team; b) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to reconcile their records and reports, exert effort to identify the unaccounted PPE located by locating their records/reports; c) require the MGSO to maintain complete and updated Property Cards and renew the PARs every three years; d) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Schedule of Accumulated Depreciation and Other Assets (for unserviceable PPE) which had matched to the total recorded PPE; e) provide depreciation for Other Land Improvements account of GF.
observations.reason: Non-completion of physical inventory by the Office of the MGSO. Unavailability of previous records/reports. Incomplete PCs and PARs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to: a) prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant the list of taxpayers with delinquencies for years 2002 to 2017 as of December 31, 2017; and b) prepare a statement of the reason/s for such non-collection and submit to the Municipal Accountant. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a) prepare the necessary adjustments of RPT/SET Receivables once the list of delinquent taxpayers is received from the MT to reflect the correct balance of these receivable accounts; and b) maintain subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer to facilitate monitoring and preparation of necessary reports/schedules.
observations.managementAction: Furnished the Municipal Accountant the list of taxpayers with delinquencies for year from CYs 2002 to 2017 to the Municipal Accountant. Wrote a letter to Office of the SB requesting for assistance for the enforcement of remedies provided in RA No. 7160. Made adjustments based on the Summary of Receivables once the list of delinquent taxpayers submitted by the MT. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the list of individual taxpayers with delinquencies.
observations.reason: The computer records of the MT will also be used as necessary reports/schedules; subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer was not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO and Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the regulations/procedures on the proper accounting, recording and management of inventories in accordance with the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs in order to have reliable balances of inventories and relative expenses and to avoid improper utilization and wastage of the same. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the MGSO with stock room to facilitate compliance with the regulation on proper recording and reporting of the receipt and disposal of inventories.
observations.managementAction: Exerted effort to identify all Contracts. Maintained PCs and PARs for some PPE. The MGSO committed to buy a container van to be used as storage for Municipal supplies. Pending availability of storage, the inventories will be issued to concerned offices which will maintain their own stock cards or logbook and will prepare the RPCI for consolidation. Municipal Accountant committed to comply.
observations.reason: Unavailability of storage, incomplete implementation; still no stock room for supplies and materials; some practices (recording as expenses) persisted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor create an appraisal committee/team to properly appraise the donated land as to their fair values at the time of acquisition and apply the same to purchased land if the acquisition costs cannot be determined reliably. We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) MGSO to follow-up the request from the RD regarding the owner’s copy of title for the seven parcels of land; and b) Municipal Assessor and MGSO to facilitate the titling of the 12 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality by making available the Deed of Donation/Deed of Sale and other pertinent documents.
observations.managementAction: Created the Appraisal Committee. Made various follow-ups to the RD. Obtained a copy of title for one parcel of land. Municipal Assessor made various communications to the CENRO.
observations.reason: The reclassification/adjustment and full titling work still ongoing for remaining parcels; some records still to be secured from RD.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MT, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and Municipal Agriculturist to continue adopting efficient and aggressive collection strategies to enforce collection of the loan receivable accounts from the concerned debtors.
observations.managementAction: Collected a total amount of P10,850.00. The Municipal Mayor committed to extend assistance to the MT in collecting from the debtors. The MT stated he is steadfast in reaching out to the debtors/borrowers during Bisita Official and collaborating with MAO and barangay officials for fast settlement.
observations.reason: Death, change of residence and physical disability of beneficiaries; unavailability of records for earlier years; low collection efficiency continues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Municipal officials to submit to her office a report on the status of the Computerization Program-Phases I and II, afterwards, make a written communication to the service provider requiring the latter to complete the system programs and fix the noted gaps. Otherwise, the total payments of P7,008,891.00 will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Wrote a letter to the service provider with supporting status reports from concerned Municipal Officials. The Municipal Accountant requested additional time and justification to fix noted gaps; hardware components and some modules are functional. Continuous communication with service provider to address issues.
observations.reason: Defects/gaps were still not addressed by the service provider; some modules remain incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) create a Disposal Committee; b) prioritize the immediate sale of unserviceable properties through public auction or other modes of disposal to avoid further deterioration of the same and earn at least the salvage value of these assets and to unclog the very limited office space/storage of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Created the Disposal Committee; identified additional unserviceable PPE. MGSO committed to comply and stated some items were placed in open space due to inadequate storage.
observations.reason: Still not disposed of; inadequate storage space and incomplete documentation; some items placed in open space and vulnerable to deterioration/theft.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to expedite the implementation of all projects programmed under the 20% DF so that intended beneficiaries avail the maximum benefits that could be derived therefrom and require the Bids and Awards Committee to find solutions to the problems causing delays.
observations.managementAction: Identified the low rate of market fees as one cause of low income. Various projects programmed in CYs 2017 and 2018 were identified; BAC and MPDC to find solutions.
observations.reason: Various procurement problems (failed biddings, lack of interested suppliers/contractors, incomplete eligibility) continue; deliberation on proposals (e.g., market fee rates) pending with Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a) refund the P86,620.28 as liquidated damages and require concerned supplier/contractor to refund; and b) ensure that liquidated damages in future on late deliveries are duly recognized and deducted before payments are made to suppliers on the account of its procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: Deducted the LD on late deliveries in succeeding payments made to supplier/contractor. Municipal Accountant explained giving considerations to reasons by suppliers/end-users (unavailability of stock) but committed to stop giving considerations and strictly implement the LD.
observations.reason: Some suppliers/contractors or end-users had no succeeding transactions so LD could not be deducted; Municipal Accountant previously gave considerations which delayed strict imposition.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials concerned to identify/determine, evaluate and address the causes of the incurrence of operating loss. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustment of affected accounts of GF and LMEEU for fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Identified the low rate of market fees as one cause of low income. The Municipal Budget Officer stated GF will subsidize LMEEU for the meantime. Municipal Accountant stated transfers from GF will be recorded appropriately when funds are available.
observations.reason: No appropriation yet for such expenses under LMEEU; proposal for new market fee rates still pending with Sangguniang Bayan; subsidy from GF being used in interim.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to comply strictly with the regulation by depositing all collections promptly and intact.
observations.managementAction: Deposited promptly and intact all collections. Municipal Treasurer reported strict compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) Municipal Health Officer to ensure that all requested drugs and medicines are included in the lists provided by the PNDF, and if not, secure authority from Head of the National Center for Pharmaceutical Management and Access for the purchased items; otherwise, refund the total amount of P83,687.66; and b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the requirements for the purchase of drugs and medicines are complied with before disbursing government funds.
observations.managementAction: Made sure that the requested drugs and medicines are included in the lists provided by the Philippine National Formulary. Stopped the dispersal of small animals. Amount of P83,687.66 was refunded. Municipal Accountant verified PNDF compliance.
observations.reason: Some purchases required issuance of Notice of Disallowance previously; partial compliance and refunds effected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to: a) complete the conduct of the physical count/inventory of the animals and prepare and render the report thereon complete with supporting documents as proof of the status/condition of animals; b) remind the beneficiaries of their obligations as stipulated in the Animal Dispersal Contract; c) conduct a complete investigation on the circumstances surrounding the death of the animals and if negligence was committed, compel the concerned recipients to refund the cost of animals; d) identify the recipients of the transferred animal as next beneficiaries; and e) prove with evidence the allegation of the non-receipt of 22 swine by beneficiaries. We also recommended that the MT must be required to collect the cost of the swine from its beneficiaries in accordance with the dispersal contract and refrain from implementing similar projects if sustainability is not assured.
observations.managementAction: Exerted effort to find all Contracts. Conducted physical inventory for animals dispersed in CYs 2015 and 2017. Reminded beneficiaries of those dispersed in CYs 2015 and 2017. Stopped the dispersal of small animals. MT called attention to beneficiaries during Bisita Opisyal.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records for animals dispersed in CYs 2001-2004; investigation on deaths and collection/enforcement not yet fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO-Designate to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required report for the first quarter of 2018 and later submitted quarterly reports for the four quarters of CY 2018. MDRRMO committed to comply with monthly submission.
observations.reason: Not aware that a monthly report (instead of quarterly) is being required by the regulation; previously submitted quarterly reports only.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor provide the necessary support in enjoining all barangays to establish their own Material Recovery Facility (MRF) and to strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Enjoined all barangays to establish their own MRF. MENRO-Designate committed to submit actual pictures of additional barangays with MRF and to give full attention to environmental regulations in 2019.
observations.reason: Inadequate monitoring of compliance with municipal regulations; some barangays still without MRF; some photos submitted were identical/duplicated; relaxed imposition of penalties to violators.

observations.recommendation:
For reliable PPE balances, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct and complete the
physical count of the Municipality’s property and submit the
RPCPPE and IIRUP for GF, SEF and LMEEU to the Audit Team;

require the Municipal Accountant and MGSO to reconcile their
records and exert effort to identify/locate the unaccounted PPE;

require the MGSO to maintain complete PCs and renew the PARs
every three years;

d. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit
Team the complete schedule of recorded PPE; and

e. provide depreciation for Other Land Improvements account of GF.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Treasurer to:

a. prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant the list of taxpayers
with delinquencies for years 2002 to 2017 as of December 31, 2017;
and

b. prepare a statement of the reason/s for such non-collection and submit
to the Sanggunian Bayan (SB) together with the list of taxpayers with
delinquencies requesting for assistance in the enforcement of the
remedies for collection provided in RA No. 7160 for the Municipality
to have funds for other projects that will benefit its constituents.

We further reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments of
RPT/SET Receivables once the list of delinquent taxpayers is received from the
Municipal Treasurer to reflect the correct balance of these receivable accounts,
and to maintain subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer to facilitate monitoring and

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO and
Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the regulations/procedures on the

the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs in order to have reliable balances of
inventories and relative expenses and to avoid improper utilization and wastage
of the same.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the MGSO
with stock room to facilitate compliance with the regulation on proper receipt
and disposal of inventories.
